description: Central Sector Scheme providing income support of Rs 6,000 per year to all farmer families across the country in three equal installments.
benefits: Rs 6,000 per year (Rs 2,000 every 4 months) transferred directly to bank account of eligible farmer families.
eligibility_text: All landholding farmer families with cultivable land. Must reside in rural areas. Income from farming must be primary source. Excludes institutional landholders, constitutional post holders, officers earning over Rs 10,000/month, income taxpayers, doctors, engineers, lawyers, CAs.

description: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana provides financial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage due to unforeseen events like natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
benefits: Full sum insured for crop losses. Premium rates: 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for commercial crops. Balance premium paid by government. Covers post-harvest losses for up to 14 days.
eligibility_text: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas. Compulsory for loanee farmers with KCC. Voluntary for non-loanee farmers. No income restriction.

description: Mahatma Gandhi NREGS provides 100 days of guaranteed wage employment per year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer for unskilled manual work.
benefits: 100 days of guaranteed wage employment per household per year. Wage paid as per state-notified MGNREGS wage rate. Unemployment allowance if work not provided within 15 days. Work within 5 km of residence.
eligibility_text: Any adult member of a rural household willing to do unskilled manual work. Must reside in rural area. No income restriction. Apply at local gram panchayat for Job Card.

description: Provides farmers with timely credit support for their cultivation needs, post-harvest expenses, and allied activities through a flexible credit system.
benefits: Short-term credit limit based on land holding and crop pattern. Interest rate 7% per annum (4% with prompt repayment subsidy). Credit limit valid for 5 years. Accidental insurance coverage of Rs 50,000.
eligibility_text: All farmers — individual or joint borrowers who are owner cultivators, tenant farmers, oral lessees, and sharecroppers. SHGs and JLGs of farmers are also eligible. No strict income limit.

description: PMAY-G aims to provide pucca houses with basic amenities to all houseless families and families in kutcha or dilapidated houses in rural areas.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs 1.20 lakh (plains) or Rs 1.30 lakh (hilly areas) per unit. Additional Rs 12,000 for toilet under SBM-G. 90 person-days of unskilled labour under MGNREGS.
eligibility_text: Houseless families and those in zero/one/two-room kutcha houses in rural areas. Priority to SC/ST, bonded labourers, minorities, widows, disabled, families with no adult male (16-59 yrs). Annual income not exceeding Rs 3 lakh.

description: Provides affordable housing to urban poor including EWS, LIG and MIG categories through interest subsidy on home loans.
benefits: Interest subsidy of 6.5% for EWS/LIG on loan up to Rs 6 lakh. 4% for MIG-I on loan up to Rs 9 lakh. 3% for MIG-II on loan up to Rs 12 lakh. Maximum subsidy amount varies by category.
eligibility_text: EWS: Annual income up to Rs 3 lakh. LIG: Rs 3-6 lakh. MIG-I: Rs 6-12 lakh. MIG-II: Rs 12-18 lakh. Applicant or any family member should not own a pucca house anywhere in India. First-time homebuyers.

description: World's largest health assurance scheme providing health cover of Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
benefits: Health cover of Rs 5 lakh per family per year. Cashless and paperless access at empanelled public and private hospitals. Covers 1,929 medical procedures including pre- and post-hospitalization.
eligibility_text: Families identified in SECC 2011 data. Deprived rural families and urban worker occupational categories. No family size or age restriction. Annual income must not exceed Rs 2.5 lakh.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana - State Expansion (AB-PMJAY)
description: Extended health coverage to additional families not covered under SECC 2011, under state-government top-up arrangements.
benefits: Rs 5 lakh health cover per year. Cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals. Covers pre-existing diseases from day one. No cap on family size.
eligibility_text: Varies by state. Generally targets families with annual income below Rs 5 lakh. Includes migrant workers, construction workers, street vendors, domestic workers, ASHA workers, mid-day meal workers.

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students
description: Central Government scholarship to support Scheduled Caste students in post-matriculation or post-secondary education.
benefits: Maintenance allowance Rs 380-1,200 per month by course and accommodation type. Reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees. Study tour and thesis charges for research scholars.
eligibility_text: Must belong to Scheduled Caste (SC). Annual parental income not exceeding Rs 2.5 lakh. Studying at post-matriculation level in government-recognized institution. Age 18-35 years.

scheme_name: National Scholarship Portal - OBC Pre-Matric Scholarship
description: Scholarship for Other Backward Class students studying at Class 1 to 10 in government and government-aided schools.
benefits: Day scholars: Rs 100/month (Cl 1-5), Rs 150/month (Cl 6-8), Rs 200/month (Cl 9-10). Hostellers: Rs 500/month. Ad-hoc grant up to Rs 1,000 for books and stationery.
eligibility_text: Must belong to OBC category. Annual family income not exceeding Rs 1 lakh. Studying in Class 1 to 10 in a government or government-aided school. Age not exceeding 15 years.

scheme_name: PM Scholarship Scheme for Central Armed Police Forces & Railway Protection Force
description: Scholarship for wards and widows of CAPF and RPF personnel for professional degree courses.
benefits: Rs 3,000 per month for boys and Rs 3,500 per month for girls for 1st to 5th year of professional degree courses. Annual scholarship for 5 years.
eligibility_text: Ward or widow of CAPF (CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, NIA, NSG, Assam Rifles) or RPF personnel. Minimum 60% marks in qualifying exam. Enrolled in recognized professional degree program.

scheme_name: Minority Scholarship - Maulana Azad National Fellowship
description: Fellowship for minority community students pursuing M.Phil and PhD in UGC-recognized universities.
benefits: JRF: Rs 31,000/month for first 2 years. SRF: Rs 35,000/month thereafter. Contingency: Rs 10,000/year (humanities) or Rs 12,000/year (sciences). HRA as per norms.
eligibility_text: Must belong to minority community (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Parsi). Enrolled in M.Phil/PhD at UGC-recognized institution. Annual family income not exceeding Rs 6 lakh. Age limit 30 years JRF.

scheme_name: Widow Pension - Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension (IGNWPS)
description: Social security pension for widows from BPL households under the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP).
benefits: Monthly pension Rs 300 for widows aged 40-79 years. Rs 500 per month for widows aged 80+ years. States may add supplementary amounts from their own funds.
eligibility_text: Must be a widow. Age between 40 and 79 years. Must belong to BPL household. Permanent resident of the state. No specific caste restriction.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
description: Monthly pension to senior citizens from BPL households under the National Social Assistance Programme.
benefits: Rs 200/month for persons aged 60-79 years. Rs 500/month for those aged 80 years and above. States may provide additional top-up amounts.
eligibility_text: Age 60 years or above. Must belong to BPL household as per the criteria of the state government. Permanent resident of the state. No specific income limit beyond BPL status.

scheme_name: National Handicapped Finance & Development Corporation Schemes (NHFDC)
description: Concessional loan schemes for persons with disabilities to support self-employment, education, and entrepreneurship.
benefits: Loans up to Rs 30 lakh at 5% (women) or 6% (men) interest. Repayment up to 10 years with 6-12 month moratorium. Covers small businesses, agriculture, education, and housing.
eligibility_text: Person with benchmark disability of 40% or more. Age 18-55 years. Annual family income not exceeding Rs 3 lakh (General/OBC) or Rs 3.5 lakh (SC/ST). Indian citizen.

description: Comprehensive medical care to Ex-Servicemen and their dependants through ECHS Polyclinics and empanelled hospitals.
benefits: Outpatient treatment at ECHS polyclinics. Specialist and hospitalization at empanelled hospitals. Cashless treatment for ESM and dependants. Covers all diseases including chronic ailments.
eligibility_text: Ex-Serviceman who served in Indian Army, Navy, or Air Force. Must be discharged/retired (not dismissed). Includes widows of Ex-Servicemen. Dependant family members covered. One-time subscription required.

description: Micro-finance loans to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises through MUDRA Bank.
benefits: Shishu: up to Rs 50,000. Kishore: Rs 50,001-5 lakh. Tarun: Rs 5,00,001-10 lakh. No collateral required. MUDRA Card for working capital. No processing fee for Shishu loans.
eligibility_text: Any Indian citizen with a business plan for non-farm income-generating activity. Small manufacturers, shopkeepers, fruit/vegetable vendors, artisans, service sector. No minimum age or strict income limit.

scheme_name: PM SVANidhi - Street Vendor AtmaNirbhar Nidhi
description: Micro-credit scheme to help street vendors resume livelihoods affected by COVID-19 lockdown.
benefits: Initial loan of Rs 10,000. Enhanced to Rs 20,000 on timely repayment. Further Rs 50,000 on 2nd repayment. 7% interest subsidy on timely repayment. Digital reward up to Rs 1,200/year.
eligibility_text: Street vendors vending in urban areas as on or before March 24, 2020. Must have vending certificate or ULB letter of recommendation. Occupation must be street vendor. Urban area resident.

description: Provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization.
benefits: Seed funding up to Rs 20 lakh as grant for validation and prototype development. Up to Rs 50 lakh as convertible debentures or debt for commercialization. Disbursed through DPIIT-recognized incubators.
eligibility_text: Startup recognized by DPIIT. Incorporated not more than 2 years ago at time of application. Annual turnover not exceeding Rs 100 crore. Must not have received more than Rs 10 lakh as a DPIIT seed fund.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: Term life insurance scheme providing life cover at a very low premium for death due to any reason.
benefits: Life cover of Rs 2 lakh on death from any cause. Annual premium Rs 436 only. Auto-debit from savings account. Renewable every year. Cover from June 1 to May 31.
eligibility_text: Age 18-50 years. Must have savings bank or post office account. Auto-debit consent required. No income restriction. Both rural and urban eligible. Must give health declaration.

description: Accident insurance scheme covering accidental death and disability at extremely low premium.
benefits: Rs 2 lakh for accidental death or total permanent disability. Rs 1 lakh for partial permanent disability. Annual premium just Rs 20. Auto-debit from savings account.
eligibility_text: Age 18-70 years. Must have savings bank account. Auto-debit facility needed. No income restriction. Both rural and urban residents eligible.

description: Pension scheme focused on unorganized sector workers, providing fixed minimum pension after age 60.
benefits: Fixed pension of Rs 1,000-5,000 per month after age 60 based on contribution. Spouse gets same pension after subscriber death. Nominee gets accumulated corpus on spouse death.
eligibility_text: Indian citizen aged 18-40 years. Must have savings bank/post office account. Must not be an income tax payer. Targeted at unorganized sector workers. Both rural and urban eligible.

description: Voluntary and contributory pension scheme for unorganized sector workers aged 18-40 years providing assured monthly pension of Rs 3,000 after age 60.
benefits: Assured monthly pension of Rs 3,000 after 60 years. Government matches the monthly contribution of the subscriber. Family pension of 50% (Rs 1,500) to spouse on death of pensioner.
eligibility_text: Age 18-40 years. Unorganised sector worker. Monthly income not exceeding Rs 15,000. Not covered under EPFO, ESIC, or NPS. Not an income tax assessee.

description: Small savings scheme for girl child under Beti Bachao Beti Padhao with high interest and tax benefits.
benefits: Interest rate 8.2% per annum (Q1 2024-25). Tax exemption under Section 80C. Maturity amount fully tax-free. Matures after 21 years or at girl's marriage after 18. Partial withdrawal after 18 for education.
eligibility_text: Account opened by natural/legal guardian for girl child below 10 years. Maximum two accounts per family (one per girl). No income or caste restriction. Both rural and urban eligible.

scheme_name: Ujjwala Yojana - Free LPG Connection for BPL Women (PMUY)
description: Provides clean cooking fuel (LPG) to women from BPL households who previously used firewood or cow dung cakes.
benefits: Free LPG connection including security deposit, regulator, and first-fill. EMI facility for stove and first refill. Connection in name of adult woman of BPL household.
eligibility_text: Adult woman (18+ years) from BPL household. No existing LPG connection in household. Permanent resident of India. Priority to SC/ST, PMAY-G beneficiaries, Antyodaya Anna Yojana beneficiaries.

description: Multi-sectoral scheme to prevent gender-biased sex selective elimination, ensure survival and protection, and promote education of girl child.
benefits: Educational incentives for girl child. Direct cash transfer in select states. Convergence with SSY for financial benefits. Awareness campaigns and community mobilization support.
eligibility_text: Families with girl children. Focus on districts with low child sex ratio. No income restriction. All categories and communities covered.

description: Free food grain scheme under NFSA providing 5 kg food grains per person per month to over 80 crore beneficiaries.
benefits: 5 kg of wheat or rice free per person per month. Covers over 80 crore beneficiaries. Available at ration shops across India. Now merged with NFSA as a permanent scheme.
eligibility_text: NFSA beneficiaries: Priority Households (PHH) and Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) card holders. Annual family income very low (BPL/extremely poor). State-wise identification of beneficiaries.

description: Financial assistance by Punjab Government for SC girls at time of marriage to help families avoid debt.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs 51,000 at marriage. Amount transferred directly to bank account of the girl or her guardian.
eligibility_text: Scheduled Caste (SC) applicant. Permanent resident of Punjab. Annual family income not exceeding Rs 32,790. Girl must be 18 years or above at time of marriage.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)
description: Monthly pension to persons with severe/multiple disabilities from BPL households under NSAP.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs 300 for persons aged 18-79 with severe disability (80%+). Rs 500/month for those aged 80+. States may add supplementary amounts.
eligibility_text: Person with severe or multiple disabilities (80% or more). Age 18-79 years. BPL household. Permanent state resident. No specific category restriction.

scheme_name: Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase of Aids & Appliances (ADIP)
description: Government scheme to assist persons with disabilities in procuring durable, sophisticated, scientifically manufactured aids and appliances.
benefits: Free or subsidized assistive devices such as wheelchair, hearing aids, artificial limbs, tricycles, braille kits, Daisy players. Devices worth up to Rs 10,000 for income up to Rs 15,000/month.
eligibility_text: Indian citizen with disability certified by government medical authority. Annual income not exceeding Rs 2 lakh. Devices provided once every 3 years. Priority to children, women, and SC/ST.

description: Flagship skill development scheme enabling youth to take up industry-relevant skill training for better livelihood opportunities.
benefits: Free short-term skill training (150-300 hours). Certification recognized by industry. Monetary reward after successful assessment and certification. Placement assistance post-training.
eligibility_text: Indian national aged 15-45 years. Unemployed or school/college dropout. Must have basic literacy. No income restriction. Both rural and urban eligible. SC/ST and minority candidates preferred for reserved seats.
